title: What's the through-line from my OSU CS coursework to what I'm building now?
html:
Question

"What's the through-line from my OSU CS coursework to what I'm building now?"

Synthesized answer

The through-line isn't "CS taught me to code." It's that the last three semesters of OSU were a stealth product spec for what Yahir would build right after. Each capstone-era topic became a load-bearing piece of Rankett within nine months of the final.

The cleanest causal arc — IR class to Rankett

The Jan 10, 2025 Information Retrieval notes are a scrap on web crawler design — spider traps, politeness toward target sites. Three months later he starts AEO; nine months later Rankett launches. Per cs coursework: "Yahir studied search relevance the semester before he started selling brand-relevance-in-LLMs." Schema markup, ranking signals, citation graphs — all undergrad IR content, repointed at LLMs instead of Google. The OSU CS canvas's right-most insight node literally says "IR coursework is the conceptual bridge."

The reverse-engineering instinct, repurposed

Per reverse engineering (densified 2026-04-27): "the take-it-apart skill mirrors Yahir's instinct to disassemble market positioning — aeo agency competitive research moves use the same energy." The May 19, 2025 IDA Pro marathon (10+ screenshots in a single afternoon) and the libwebp CVE reading on May 14 trained a posture — binaries don't lie, read what's actually there — that he now applies to competitor LLM-visibility audits.

The security capstone wasn't a detour, it was the engineer-identity hardening

Per osu: the April 27, 2025 Jain/Pankanti/Prabhakar biometrics survey screenshots, the June 1 Felt et al. UC Berkeley mobile malware survey (46 samples, credential black-market price sheet, first 2FA-via-SMS bypass), and the June 10 Wireshark capture hunting /ned.exe at 192.168.157.134 — that's the semester Yahir stopped being a CS student and started being someone who expects every system has an exploitable seam. That's the Rankett worldview: every ranking system has a seam an operator can work.

The deepest thread — already spec'd in 2022, before the coursework even mattered

Per the existing query ahmad jamal to rankett throughline: the Oct 25, 2022 Ahmad Jamal mega dump — the same week as CS 162, CS 271, ECON 830, and the "figure out while loop" note — already names all three Rankett premises (asking the right question, nonobvious, "1/10 resources" compression). The CS coursework didn't generate the thesis. It built the engineer who could ship it.

The engineer at graduation

From the OSU CS canvas insight node, unchanged:

"Not a generalist programmer — a (1) information-retrieval-literate (2) reverse-engineering-instinct (3) ML-curious + (4) software-testing-disciplined engineer. Each layer maps to a distinct semester of the OSU CS arc."

The through-line in one sentence: OSU CS gave Yahir four capstone-grade primitives — IR, RE, RL, software testing — and Rankett is what happens when the IR semester meets the Ahmad-Jamal-stream worldview the same person was already running in 2022.

The bookend you may have forgotten

2022 10 13 figure out while loop (Oct 13, 2022) — a title-only note, empty body, three and a half years old. Two days before the Ahmad Jamal mega-dump where Yahir workshopped LLM applications weeks after ChatGPT launched. The summary's why-it-matters reads:

"Three years before rankett, the same person who'd architect a multi-tenant white-label platform was stuck on while. Yahir's technical instincts in 2026 are not native; they're earned."

That's the bookend. The same October week he couldn't iterate a loop, he wrote down the three premises that became his company. The CS coursework is what closed the gap between those two facts.

Sources

  • OSU CS coursework arc canvas — primary visual artifact
  • cs coursework · osu — concept/entity layer
  • ahmad jamal to rankett throughline — sister query, philosophical axis
  • 2025 01 10 Information Retreival Notes — IR class scrap
  • reverse engineering · ida pro · busybox · webp cve · malware analysis lab · network forensics · reinforcement learning — capstone topics
  • 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream — origin polymath dump
  • 2022 10 13 figure out while loop — the bookend
  • rankett · aeo agency · ai search optimization — downstream applications
